mnemonics.title: ADD vs ADDC Memory Aid
mnemonics.content: ADD = basic addition; ADDC = Add with Carry. Remember: C in ADDC = Carry, so use ADDC when propagating carry in multi-byte math.

mnemonics.title: MUL and DIV Flag Behavior
mnemonics.content: MUL: C=0 always, OV=1 if B≠0 after. DIV: C=0 always, OV=1 only if divide by zero. Remember: Carry always cleared in both.

mnemonics.title: Logical Instructions Flag Rule
mnemonics.content: ANL/ORL/XRL: C not affected (stays same), AC affected, OV undefined. Arithmetic: all flags affected. Remember: Logical preserves Carry!

mnemonics.title: DA Instruction Correction
mnemonics.content: DA adds: 0x06 if AC=1 and upper nibble<10; 0x60 if C=1 and lower nibble<10; 0x66 if both AC=1 and C=1 and both nibbles<10. Think: Fix lower, then fix upper.

mnemonics.title: Rotate Through Carry vs Simple Rotate
mnemonics.content: RL/RR = Simple rotate (no carry involved). RLC/RRC = rotate through Carry. Remember: The 'C' in RLC/RRC stands for Carry.

acronyms.usage: 8051 uses CISC architecture with variable-length instructions (1-3 bytes) optimized for embedded control.
8-bit SFR containing flags: CY (carry), AC (auxiliary carry), OV (overflow), P (parity).
Encoding where each decimal digit (0-9) is represented by 4 binary bits. 0x59 BCD = decimal 59.
The 8051's ALU performs arithmetic (ADD, SUBB, MUL, DIV) and logical (ANL, ORL, XRL) operations.
128-byte area (0x80-0xFF) containing 8051 peripheral registers like ACC, B, PSW, P0-P3 ports.

visualTips.visualization: Draw 4-bit binary addition: 0111 + 0100 = 1011 (no overflow), but 0111 + 0101 = 1100 (carry from bit2→bit3, overflow since positive+positive=negative). OV = C6 XOR C7.
Imagine a 9-bit circular shift register: [C|bit7|bit6|bit5|bit4|bit3|bit2|bit1|bit0]. For RLC: bit7→C, C→bit0, all bits shift left. Visualize as a conveyor belt loop.
Two-stage correction: Lower nibble check (if >9 or AC=1, add 0x06) → Upper nibble check (if >9 or C=1, add 0x60). Think of it as two independent decimal corrections.
Column addition: Add lowest bytes with ADD (or ADDC with C=0), propagate carry with ADDC for each subsequent byte. Show: [Byte2][Byte1][Byte0] + carry flowing upward.
RAM 0x20-0x2F (16 bytes × 8 bits = 128 addressable bits) plus 16 SFR bits (like P0.0-P0.7 at 0x80). Each byte has bits .0 to .7. Direct bit address: 0x00-0x7F for RAM bits.

analogies.analogy: Adding multi-digit numbers on paper: you add units (least significant), write result, note carry. Then add tens + carry using ADDC. Each byte is like each digit - ADDC propagates the 'carry' between them.
MUL is like finding area of rectangle: width (A) × height (B) = area. Result spans two registers (low area in A, high area in B). If height >255, overflow (B>0 after).
SWAP is like swapping the tens and units digits of a two-digit number: 37 becomes 73. In hex, each digit is 0-F, so 0xAB becomes 0xBA.
AND is like a filter mask (1=keep, 0=clear): A AND 0x0F keeps only lower nibble. OR is like combining flags: A OR 0x80 sets MSB. XOR detects differences: identical bytes XOR to 0.
CPL is like inverting colors in an image: white becomes black, black becomes white. Every bit flips: 0→1, 1→0. The result is the bitwise complement (ones complement).
